title: cratoxylum arborescens
intro: Cratoxylum arborescens, also known as the 'forest gum,' is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Hypericaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an anti-cancer agent. Cratoxylum arborescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat various ailments.
